prompt: 이 Claude Code 플러그인에 설정 validation 기능을 추가하고 싶어. .process-todos.json 파일을 읽을 때 스키마 검증하고, 잘못된 설정이면 에러 메시지를 보여줘. JSON Schema나 Zod 쓸 수 있을 것 같은데 알아서 판단해줘.

baseline.name: appropriate-file-count
baseline.description: Creates 2-3 todo files (schema definition, integration, polish)

baseline.name: correct-markdown-format
baseline.description: Each file has: H1 title, context paragraph, and checklist with '- [ ]' items

baseline.name: checklist-items-are-concrete
baseline.description: Each checklist item describes a specific implementable action, not vague directives

discriminating.name: references-project-artifacts
discriminating.description: Checklist items reference the project's actual artifacts (e.g., SKILL.md files, agents/*.md, plugin.json, .process-todos.json) rather than defaulting to src/lib/ or TypeScript file paths

discriminating.name: adapts-to-non-code-project
discriminating.description: Context paragraphs or checklist items acknowledge that this is a markdown/JSON plugin with no TypeScript runtime, and adapt recommendations accordingly (e.g., choosing JSON Schema over Zod, or noting that validation happens in skill instructions rather than compiled code)

discriminating.name: includes-validation-approach
discriminating.description: At least one todo specifies the validation mechanism with reasoning — not just 'add validation' but HOW validation works in a project without a code runtime
